text: Difficult setup/configuration of devices (e.g., alarm clock)

High-quality audio and sound clarity
Platform glitches prevent content from being seen (videos stuck at 0 views, in review for hours)

Contacting customer service and receiving no response to purchase issues.

Responsive and effective customer service for issue resolution.

Disappointment when paid features malfunction or lack expected content (e.g., missing graphics, broken updates)
Reliable app performance with consistent functionality after updates
Intuitive, user-friendly interfaces that enhance rather than hinder experience
App crashes or becomes unusable after updates, especially beta versions
Difficulty spikes in games that feel unfair or abruptly block progress

Reliable, crash-free app performance, especially for core functions

Service termination due to opaque 'excessive use' policies with no clear recourse
Inadequate customer support channels (no phone support, only online chat)
Loss of paid service time and money upon termination

Frustration with low organic reach and algorithm changes on Instagram
Feeling forced into a pay-to-play model on social platforms
Disappointment when tech products fail after initial success

Frustration with sudden algorithm changes drastically reducing organic reach/engagement
Anxiety over losing followers and audience despite consistent effort
Feeling platform intentionally suppresses content to force paid promotions
Consistent, reliable growth in followers and engagement
Mastery of platform algorithms to maximize organic reach
Here we go again with pricing that's not upfront. If I have to create an account just to see what this costs, that's already a red flag that there are going to be surprise charges later.
Okay, they have good reviews but let me check specifically for mentions of crashes or data loss. I can't afford another app that's going to lose my work.
This navigation is already confusing and I'm just trying to find basic information. If I can't figure out how to use their website, their app is probably going to be just as poorly designed.
Another signup form asking for way too many permissions. Do they really need access to my contacts just to let me post photos? This feels like a data grab.
Finally, an app that actually does what it says and hasn't crashed in two days. Maybe I can actually rely on this one for my content creation work.
